solutions.solution: (b) From positions I and III. Common letter = T (at the same face). Clearly, R is opposite to Q.
(d) From positions I and III. Common dots = 1 and 3. Clearly, 6 dots is opposite to 5 dots.
(d) From positions I and II. Common digits = 1 and 3. Clearly, 6 is opposite to 4.
(c) On unfolding the dice. From the above unfolded dice we find that 6 is opposite to face containing 3.
(d) From positions I and IV. Adjacent faces of 4 = 2, 3, 5, 6. Clearly, 1 is opposite to 4.
(c) From positions I and IV. Common dots = 2 (at the same face). Clearly, 1 is opposite to 6.
(d) From positions I and II. Common digits = 3 and 5. Clearly, 1 is opposite to 4.
(d) From positions I and IV. Common dots = 2 (at the same face). Clearly, 1 dot is opposite to 4 dots.

(b) According to the question, So, circle is opposite to blank square, circle is opposite to blank square and hatched square is opposite to hatched square. Hence, only the cube in figure (b) is correct.

(d) According to the unfolded dice, So, blank square is opposite to blank square, F is opposite to black square and blank square is opposite to black square. Clearly, only option (a) is having the exact cube which can be formed when unfolded cube is folded.
(c) According to the question, So, blank square is opposite to blank square, circle is opposite to square and blank square is opposite to blank square. Hence, figure III and IV can be formed.
(c) According to the question, So, circle is opposite to blank square, + is opposite to blank square and @ is opposite to blank square. Hence, all the cube figures can be formed.
(b) According to the question, So, hatched square is opposite to square, blank square is opposite to circle and blank square is opposite to @. Hence, only cubes I and III can be formed.

(c) According to the question, So, blank square is opposite to circle, blank square is opposite to blank square and blank square is opposite to blank square. Clearly, only cubes I and IV can be formed when unfolded cube is folded.
(c) From both the positions I and II. Common figure = circle (at the same face). Clearly, + is opposite to diamond.
(d) From both the positions I and II. Common dots = 2 and 4. Hence, 6 dots will be opposite to 3 dots. Clearly, 6 will be at the bottom, if 3 dots are at top face.
(c) From both the positions I and II. Common dots = 3 and 6. Hence, 1/5 dots will be opposite to 3 dots. Clearly, 1/5 dots will be at the bottom face, if 3 dots are at the top face.
(c) From the two views of cube it is clear that '3' lies opposite to '1'.
